title: Fail-closed composite Event Timing replay acceptance gate

primary_sources.relevance: Defines Event Timing entries, interaction grouping, and observer capability assumptions used by the capability layer.
Defines PerformanceEntry timestamps relative to a performance time origin.
Defines performance.now() and performance.timeOrigin on a monotonic clock domain.

finding: Individually valid capability, navigation-freshness, and monotonic-clock packets do not create a safe replay decision unless one fail-closed gate requires every layer and binds them to the same opaque navigation identity.
design_implication: Downstream replay interpretation must consume only replay_acceptance_valid from the composite gate. It must not independently cherry-pick a successful lower-level packet while another layer is unresolved or rejected.

verification.runtime: isolated Node.js execution using byte-equivalent gate logic and repository dependency behavior
cases: raw clock and event fields excluded from retained output

observed: R-014, R-015, and R-016 expose separate capability, integration, and clock-binding decisions.
Seven deterministic composite-gate tests passed in an isolated Node.js runtime.
inferred: Without a single acceptance boundary, a consumer can accidentally treat one successful lower-level packet as sufficient evidence.
proposed: Vercel Studio should invoke this gate immediately before replay interpretation and expose only its bounded output.
superseded: Allowing downstream consumers to combine lower-level Event Timing evidence ad hoc.
unresolved: No deployed Vercel path invokes the composite gate.
No physical iPhone Safari backgrounding, reload, or wall-clock-adjustment packet was evaluated.

falsification_route: Supply one accepted layer and one rejected layer and verify the composite result remains unresolved and false.
Reuse packets across reloads with matching wall-clock freshness but different time origins and verify rejection.
Background physical iPhone Safari beyond the monotonic budget and verify stale rejection before replay interpretation.
